name: Aira Caldera
eruptionHistory: The massive caldera hosts Sakurajima volcano. A catastrophic eruption 22,000 years ago ejected over 150 km³ of material.
description: Aira is a massive caldera in southern Kyushu that formed ~22,000 years ago. Sakurajima volcano sits within the caldera and is one of Japan's most active volcanoes.
